name: advance-codex-dev
description: [DEV] Capability plugin for strengthening Codex surfaces across skills, scenario testing, session folder conventions, commits, plugins, tool-use guidance, and subagent systems.

interface.shortDescription: [DEV] Strengthen skills, scenario testing, session folder conventions, commits, tool-use guidance, plugins, and Codex subagent systems.
interface.longDescription: [DEV] Provides skills for strengthening Codex surfaces: shaping reusable skills, testing reusable instructions with fixed scenarios and fresh executors, defining session folder conventions, finalizing Codex-made changes into stable commits, isolating tool-use policy, packaging plugins, preparing bounded subagent handoffs, running reviewable work units through worker subagent lifecycles, and designing reliable custom agents.
defaultPrompt: Use $advance-codex-dev:plugin-creator to package a plugin and define the right bundled surfaces.
Use $advance-codex-dev:skill-creator to design a reusable Codex skill with the right references, guardrails, and description trigger metadata.
Use $advance-codex-dev:skill-scenario-testing to test reusable instructions with fresh subagents, fixed scenarios, and caller-side metrics before reporting evidence-backed findings.
Use $advance-codex-dev:agents-sessions to define when artifacts belong under date-based .agents/sessions/{YYYYMMDD} instead of source, spec, or release surfaces.
Use $advance-codex-dev:git-committer to turn Codex-made changes into a task-scoped commit with strong verification and commit-message hygiene.
Use $advance-codex-dev:tool-use-guide to design reusable guidance for selecting and sequencing tools without burying tool policy inside a domain skill.
Use $advance-codex-dev:subagent-gate to prepare a subagent handoff with an exit plan, minimal context packet, ownership boundary, and approval limits before spawn or message.
Use $advance-codex-dev:subagent-work to run a reviewable work unit through a strict worker subagent lifecycle: spawn, operate, verify, summarize, and close.
Use $advance-codex-dev:subagent-creator to design a Codex subagent workflow, custom agent spec, and runtime control plan.
